content.problemStatement:
Chef has two positive integers XXX and YYY. Now Chef wants to perform some number of operations (possibly zero) on them. In each operation, Chef can choose either XXX or YYY and increment it by 111. Find the minimum number of operations Chef needs to perform so that there is a positive integer strictly greater than 111 which divides both XXX and YYY (In other words, the greatest common divisor of XXX and YYY should be greater than 111).


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of operations Chef needs to perform.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated positive integers X and Y.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the minimum number of operations required so that the greatest common divisor of the resulting numbers is greater than 1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: gcd(4,16)=4>1, so 0 operations.
Test case 2: gcd(4,55)=1. Adding 111 to Y gives (4,166) whose gcd is 2>1, so only 1 operation is needed.

solution.approach:
The operation adds 111 to either X or Y. We only need the final GCD to be > 1. Observe:
1. If gcd(X, Y) > 1, no operation is needed.
2. Otherwise, try a single operation. Adding 111 to X yields numbers (X+111, Y). If gcd(X+111, Y) > 1, one operation suffices. The same check is done for adding 111 to Y.
3. If neither of the above succeeds, two operations always work: add 111 to both numbers. After two additions the numbers become (X+111, Y+111). Their difference is unchanged, but at least one of the numbers is now even (since 111 is odd), guaranteeing a common divisor 2. Hence the answer is at most 2.
The algorithm therefore checks the three GCD conditions in O(log min) time per test case.
Time Complexity: O(T · log max(X,Y))
Space Complexity: O(1)
